vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #45

plan.title: Displayed temperature can be stale: doesn't refetch as you move while riding

narratives.Description: Displayed temperature can be stale: doesn't refetch as you move while riding
narratives.Overview:
## Report
The app showed **89°F** while a car thermometer read **80°F** — a ~9° gap. Question was whether the app temperature is stale.

## Investigation
Three contributing factors:

1. **No refetch on movement.** `WeatherController.updateLocation(latitude:longitude:)` stores the new coordinate but only triggers an immediate fetch on the **first** fix:
   ```swift
   func updateLocation(latitude: Double, longitude: Double) {
       let isFirstFix = coordinate == nil
       coordinate = (latitude, longitude)
       if isFirstFix { fetch() }   // later moves wait for the periodic timer
   }
   ```
   So while riding, the temperature reflects wherever you were at the last refresh, not your current location.

2. **15-minute refresh cadence.** `standardRefreshInterval = 900s` (240s only when near/below freezing). Foregrounding does force a refresh (`onScenePhaseChange(.active) -> weatherController.start() -> fetch()`), but during a continuous ride the reading can be up to ~15 minutes (and many miles) old.

3. **Modeled vs. sensor.** Open-Meteo `current.temperature_2m` is a gridded model value, not a physical station/vehicle sensor, so it will never exactly match a car thermometer (microclimates, sun-baked roads, etc.).

Factors 1–2 mean the displayed temp is effectively **stale with respect to your current location while moving**, which plausibly explains most of the gap if the ride started somewhere warmer.

## Possible improvements (to discuss)
- Refetch when the rider has moved a meaningful distance since the last weather fetch (e.g. a distance threshold like ~5–10 km), in addition to the periodic timer — while still respecting Open-Meteo's free-tier limits.
- Optionally shorten the standard cadence (e.g. 5–10 min) while actively riding.
- Optionally surface a "last updated" timestamp / freshness indicator so the value's age is transparent.
- Document that the temperature is a regional modeled value and may differ from a vehicle sensor.
narratives.Scope: Implementing two of the proposed improvements: (1) refetch weather after the rider moves a meaningful distance since the last fetch; (2) use a faster refresh cadence while actively riding. Not implementing the 'last updated' indicator in this scope.
narratives.FunctionalRequirements:
FR-1: Weather MUST refetch when the current location is at least a significant distance (default ~5 km) from the coordinate of the last fetch.
FR-2: While the rider is actively moving, the periodic refresh cadence MUST be faster than the idle/standard cadence.
FR-3: The near-or-below-freezing cadence remains the fastest and continues to take precedence.
FR-4: API usage MUST stay well within Open-Meteo free-tier limits (no per-update fetching; distance-gated + bounded cadence).
narratives.Architecture: In WeatherController: track lastFetchCoordinate (set when a fetch starts) and detect movement from the existing coordinate stream. On updateLocation, refetch if distance from lastFetchCoordinate >= significantMoveMeters; otherwise reschedule the timer so the cadence reflects current riding state. Add a pure static haversine distanceMeters helper (unit-testable). desiredInterval precedence: nearFreezing (240s) > riding (ridingRefreshInterval, e.g. 300s) > standard (900s). Riding is inferred from recent movement (consecutive-update step >= movementThresholdMeters within ridingIdleTimeout); inject a now: () -> Date clock for deterministic tests.
narratives.TestingStrategy: Add tests: refetch occurs after a >5km move and not after a small move; activeRefreshInterval becomes ridingRefreshInterval after movement and reverts to standard after the idle timeout (using injected clock). Keep existing cadence/refetch tests green. xcodebuild test + SwiftLint strict.

narrative.Acceptance: Refetch after meaningful move; faster cadence while moving; freezing still fastest; tests green.

references.title: Issue #45: Displayed temperature can be stale: doesn't refetch as you move while riding
